content_preview.title: OpenAI Rolls Out GPT-5.5-Cyber to Vetted Defenders, a Month After Mocking Anthropic's Mythos as 'Fear-Based Marketing'
content_preview.summary: OpenAI launched GPT-5.5-Cyber on May 7, 2026 to vetted security teams via its Trusted Access for Cyber program, after CEO Sam Altman publicly criticized Anthropic's restricted Mythos rollout.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

OpenAI on May 7, 2026 began rolling out GPT-5.5-Cyber, a variation of its latest frontier model trained to be more permissive on security-related tasks, to a small group of vetted cybersecurity teams, [The Decoder](https://the-decoder.com/openai-opens-gpt-5-5-cyber-to-vetted-security-researchers/) and [CNBC](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/05/07/openai-rolls-out-new-gpt-5point5-cyber-to-vetted-cybersecurity-teams.html) reported. The release lands roughly a month after Anthropic gave a sim...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).

editor_notes.content_quality: Strong, well-attributed 803-word News piece. Clean section structure, careful separation of partner-list vs. capability framing vs. benchmark numbers, and an analysis section that explicitly frames the OpenAI/Anthropic convergence rather than picking sides.
editor_notes.source_verification: Read all 5 snapshots from disk. The Decoder (source-0) confirms launch partners (Cisco, CrowdStrike, Palo Alto, Cloudflare, Intel, Snyk, SentinelOne), Codex Security program, AISI test ('2 out of 10' / '3 out of 10' attack-chain runs verified verbatim), June 1 phishing-resistant authentication requirement. TechCrunch (source-1) confirms 'fear-based marketing' verbatim, 'thousands of verified defenders', 'critical cyber defenders' verbatim, unauthorized-group-gained-access to Mythos. Help Net Security (source-2) confirms 'most permissive version' verbatim, 'not intended to significantly increase cyber capability' verbatim, and Altman's full quote 'We'd like to help companies secure themselves, and we think it's important to start work on this quickly' verified verbatim. CNBC (source-3) hit a login wall but its claims are corroborated by other sources. Axios (source-4) was fetched from Archive.org fallback; content matches.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Every factual specific traces to its cited authority. The two cross-references to prior published articles (TAC framework, Firefox 271 vulns) both exist in the archive (verified at src/content/articles/2026-02/10-... and src/content/articles/2026-04/22-...).
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral. The automated 'loaded language' flag triggered on the phrase 'the most clearly malicious workflows' in the Analysis section. In context this is a measured qualifier (denoting an unambiguous category — credential theft, attacks on third parties, which the article previously specified) rather than editorializing. Editor judgment: not a real issue.
editor_notes.originality: No archive collision. Direct successor to the GPT-5.4-Cyber piece from 2026-04-15 and Anthropic Mythos coverage from 2026-04-20.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Clean APPROVE in editor judgment, despite the automated loaded-language false positive. Multi-sourced, all direct quotes verified verbatim, careful provenance trail, neutral framing.
